title: Aboriginal People Gain Political Power | PilbaraStrike
content: strikers increasingly able negotiate individual agreements pastoralists return station work increased wages improved living conditions, department native affairs found unable reimpose control marrngu fear employment would re-join strike. letter, written tommy sampie response government decision declare twelve mile prohibited area aboriginal people camp, caused prohibition quickly reversed illustrates considerable political power marrngu gaining actions. aboriginal people gain political power transcript 23 inst. sergeant came camp made inquiries natives gathered around near sergeant, signed names completed part told us pull houses humpies. please explain. always thought native affairs would protect us every way give us home 12 mile many boys went back squatter work going stir whole thing dont blame us, fighting rights childrens, must defend personal rights ourselfs, protectors wont us, want rights freedom liberty country. since born almost say natives never home live enjoy never, since went strike, walked 12 mile remained till last sunday heard protector closing camp. dont think fair side boys girls went back work squatters causing us trouble instead helping us. five station got working, men women working today, stations notified time 12 mile camp went work. thought we’d friend government squatters, got one help us see get home young children ourselfs. might well gave natives together till find way protect us manner please every natives pilbarra district, government, native affairs never helped us, asked give us home, went us fought squatters rights. going shifted squatters get cheap labors, another thing, find home stations camp closed, beg behalf stn boys, guarantee 12 mile camp remain intact. tommy sampie, incomplete letter department native affairs, 26 january 1949, srowa, 1948/0732/86-87.

title: Anti-Fascist League | PilbaraStrike
content: anti-fascist league anti-fascist league western australian organisation founded edward (bill) beeby 1940. league activities conducted weekly radio broadcasts human rights civil liberties issues presented beeby socialists. broadcasts served focus discussion branches established throughout western australia war years. league member remembered organised listeners’ groups gathered around wirelesses sunday evening hear bill beeby speak, ‘marvelled knowledge words wisdom’. mcleod member league 1943 worked comet gold mine near marble bar, becoming marble bar branch secretary well participating comet mine discussion group. aboriginal issues included broadcasts discussions, talks given anthropologist fred rose, example. mcleod’s interest cause aboriginal rights may well stimulated discussions. wrote 1943 marble bar branch resolved every means’ ‘endeavour assist coloured people attempt gain equality social economic political’. 1943 1944 wrote articles aboriginal issues league’s newspaper, fremantle district sentinel. fostered interest league among aboriginal people port hedland, 1943 port hedland’s euralian association became affiliated organization. probably involvement anti-fascist league brought mcleod contact perth-based communists katharine susannah prichard, led joining australian communist party.

title: Clancy McKenna | PilbaraStrike
content: clancy mckenna clancy mckenna (warntupungkarna) born 1909, son pastoralist maurice mckenna nellie (nyamalangu), nyamal woman worked maurice mckenna’s station. probably born meentheena station, clancy grew coongan (also called bungalow) pilbara stations. worked various jobs around marble bar areas, including fencing well-sinking contractor, gold miner, truck driver. capable earning higher wages marrngu, resented inequality treatment aboriginal person. jack mcphee (wanamurraganya) described thinking man’. mckenna one early instigators strike movement, becoming involved discussions kitchener mcleod 1944. told jack mcphee, “sour bread kangaroo, old tea pay, that’s right jack. striking way, don’t want treated like dogs anymore”. particularly interested idea marrngu purchasing land. organised important meetings port hedland four-mile camp july 1945, spread word planned strike collected money buy land threatened arrest police. march 1946 dooley made final arrangements 1 may strike action. arrested 7 may attempting organise meeting representatives stations plan action. role organising strike received three-month jail sentence released late june following widespread protest. played leading role demonstrations port hedland july august 1946, became leader strike camp twelve mile. non-aboriginal observers impressed rev. peter hodge noted ‘fine qualities leadership thorough grip position’, dorothy hewett described giant strength resourcefulness good leaders’. even acting commissioner native affairs, lew mcbeath, found well spoken also intelligent’. early 1947 mckenna served two-month jail sentence role preventing party strikers returning station work. mckenna left strike movement second half 1947, taking work mundabullangana (munda) station playing part strike. joined mining cooperative heyday 1951 early 1952, left, unhappy organisational arrangements including decision pay wages cooperative members. also involved time pindan’s activities 1960s following split. mid-1970s recorded story life kingsley palmer, led book somewhere black white. died marble bar 20 august 1979.

title: Daisy Bindi | PilbaraStrike
content: daisy bindi daisy bindi nyangumarta woman became involved pilbara cooperative movement 1950s. take part strike 1946-49. worked life stock work domestic service pilbara stations, unpaid ‘housegirl’ roy hill station, south region, heard mcleod leaders speak cooperative meeting marble bar september 1951. although meeting urged station workers join cooperative, daisy decided leaving roy hill stage, persuading workers remain jobs negotiate better wages conditions, including women. wage increases promised management roy hill, improvements forthcoming women refused work. although married men offered extra 10/- per week wages wives, daisy insisted women would work without direct payment. negotiations failed secure improvements wages, daisy husband, bindi, visited nodom wolfram camp blue bar investigate viability cooperative mining alternative station employment. impressed conditions daisy arranged cooperative provide transport enable everyone leave roy hill. january 1952, nodom’s driver, murphy, cooperative member roy mackay arrived roy hill truck trailer, almost 100 people roy hill surrounding stations, including recent arrivals desert, boarded. receiving medical treatment marble bar, mostly sore eyes, people formed independent mining group daisy’s leadership, mining alluvial tin cooglegong. months later, however, joined forces nodom. although daisy initially charge work gang made people brought cooperative, group gradually became dispersed throughout larger group lost gang-boss status. like dooley time, leadership authority within group rather, status respected senior figure. visitors cooperative camps struck strong dynamic personality, many met including katharine susannah prichard, inspired tell story. remained cooperative economic hardships 1950s, telling western australian author, bert vickers, ‘ah, worth people happy work nobody push us around’. diabetic, leg amputated 1959, following split 1959-60 joined nomads faction. daisy died euremia 1962.

title: Norman Tindale Writes on Mining Activities | PilbaraStrike
content: physical anthropologist norman b. tindale spent three months mining community 1953 part university adelaide university california anthropological expedition 1952-54. recorded details group’s mining activities journal. norman tindale writes mining activities transcript p. 241, 5 may 1953 pigangoora. went ernie mitchell (njamal tribe) see several camps. bore camp, couple miles north along range rockhole camp, six miles away direction, pilgangoora well camp. well dug aborigines fitted weighed whip. 15 feet deep, nearly gave last dry spell yeilding well. water good. carried water firewood back camp. afternoon talked people watched mining columbite. dig old creek bed deposits 3 4 feet deep take six inches alluvial bed rock, dry swept places bank, winnow remove dust, yandy wooden often metal dishes, shaped like old wooden dishes. two dishes full original dirt, yandied produce beef tin full columbite ‘good dirt’. p. 271, 7 may evening des stuart paid visit camp much working done. learnt 1 fruit tin columbite days work member team four working patch. one digs ore, another carries picks rubbing [boards?] loosen composite pieces. yandy person, often woman, cleans separates ore. saw people work afternoon. p. 481, 10 june 1953, kulikuli creek (turkey creek) watched camp group kulikuli cleaning columbite. material brought field handed work gangs washed, dried yandied get dross, magnet drawn taking 40% iron. picking extraneous particles done material bagged export. little practice possible pick individual crystals rolled crystals rather shiny columbite distinct dull matte iron particles similar size shape. p. 487, 12 june 1953, kulikuli camp. 40 odd people kulikuli thirteen huts tents arranged two lines, one store tent, head man’s bush shelter one side. arrangement bush huts controlled class two sessions meals men mothers law keep apart. meals called ringing stock bell. breakfast sunrise, noon day meal 12:30 evening meal sunset. two work gangs go columbite; twelve members. gather daily 10-14 fruit cans cleaned ore containing iron. cleaned iron removed magnet reduces bult 60%, sometimes 40% clean columbite. work force 5-6 works every day 5-6 hours cleaning product ready bagging. camp going nearly two months. three main water pools permanent. one afternoon, columbite cleaning finished, old ladies came worked ground work spot, picking scraps ground working obviously easier play working nothing, like party women knitting something returned pilgangoora well. today saw interesting crystal form columbite work camp given collection. norman b. tindale, extracts journal ‘university adelaide university california anthropological expedition, 1952-1954’, south australian museum, aa 338-1-19-1, 241, 271, 481, 487.

we’ll fight “oh never mind”, “never mind, we’ll go. we’ll get court, we’ll catch court, we’ll he’ll buggered. won’t pull nothing”. went alright, went port hedland, sent word solicitor, mr hughes, crown lawyer perth, coming, court oh, king reg shivering everything, went like know. constable bin get name, know man? he’s crown lawyer perth, mr hughes”. a-a-after shaking, ah knock table, knock chair, everything. walk he’s tell, “walk settle come back shivering can’t, can’t get nothing. pull man, king reg, mr. hughes, “come stand want bump car, bin done two boys”. one “oh can’t”, shivering shivering. fined two hundred, two hundred pounds let go. tom de pledge nothing wrong, come clear, know, far goes. audio: paddy yabala, ordinary fence’, recorded anne scrimgeour, woodstock station, 1991. photo: paddy yabala, tim dwyer.

title: Peter Coppin | PilbaraStrike
content: peter coppin peter coppin born 1920 yarrie station nyamal woman, nurparn (sally) pastoralist, christopher coppin junior (known willie). peter grew warralong station, becoming expert stockman general station hand broad range skills. living warralong accidently shot knee white jackeroo always afterwards known ‘kangkushot’ kangku, nyamal word ‘knee’. still young man 1946, up-and-coming leader twelve mile strike. 1947 identified police reports member leadership committee following strike, coppin became leader main wolfram-mining camp cooke’s creek, 1952 charge cooperative’s mining operations pilbarra mining area, taking management nodom’s pastoral operations yandeyarra station. together active women’s committee included wife, biddy, charge community yandeyarra throughout 1953 member cooperative’s ‘top committee’ along ernie mitchell, mcleod des stuart. coppin remained group starvation time one leaders pilbara native society established 1955 department native welfare. later became director pindan, 1956 returned yandeyarra group pindan people establish vegetable gardens muster cattle support group. 1957, together mitchell mcleod, spoke public meeting perth town hall strike achievements cooperatives. remained second-in-charge pindan community split 1959, 1970, following sudden death ernie mitchell, became leader mugarinya corporation, pindan called. peter coppin recorded story life kangkushot: life nyamal lawman peter coppin (written jolly read) passed away 2006.

title: Pindan Pty Ltd | PilbaraStrike
content: pindan pty ltd pindan pty ltd formed 1955 replace failed northern development mining company. mcleod chairman board directors, directors ernie mitchell, peter coppin elsie lee, non-aboriginal supporter acted group’s purchasing agent perth. mcleod lee held one share, mitchell, coppin four marrngu (coombie, orange muthering, jacob oberdoo jacob windjaga) held 1,000 shares trust rest group. first company engaged primarily grass-seed pearl shell collection pilbara coast, 1958 turned focus mining manganese nimingarra joint enterprise albert sims co syndicate company called simdan. pindan years second half 1950s, group defiantly independent, refusing cooperate department native welfare deliberately challenging discriminatory state legislation. mid-1959 discontent among pindan members company priorities. economic prosperity early years decade remained elusive, much group’s earnings used purchase equipment company enterprises. among causes discontent, members demanded earnings returned provision better clothing, example, education children, primary objective movement still achieved. tensions came head mcleod threatened resign company retained t. j. hughes lawyer. majority members voted retain hughes anyway, mcleod accordingly resigned. following months significant portion pindan members joined mcleod form new company, called nomads pty ltd., acquired strelley station 1973. pindan continued ernie mitchell’s leadership later, mugarinya aboriginal corporation, acquired yandeyarra station.
